title: Correctness, Completeness, Optimality, Metrics, and Selection · Runtime Lens: Solver Selection
summary: A solver wins from one timing, path length is compared across different mazes, and explored-cell animation is mistaken for useful work. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the solver selection increment in solver benchmark and decision report. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose DFS, BFS, Dijkstra, or A-star from task, graph, evidence, latency, memory, pedagogy, and accessibility needs.
Compare algorithms on identical recorded mazes with warm-up, repeated trials, separated generation time, and distributions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-solver-evidence-comparison-2
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-solver-evidence-comparison-2.
# Case 7417: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def worked_maze_solver_evidence_comparison_2(fixtures: int = 24, valid_results: int = 24, solvers: int = 4, changed_cases: int = 8, isolated_metrics: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "gridworked2",
        "topology7417",
        "search2dekrow",
        "eventworked27417",
        "access7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if fixtures < 8 or valid_results < 0:
        return False, valid_results, "benchmark-fixtures"
    if valid_results != fixtures:
        return False, valid_results, "invalid-solver-result"
    if solvers < 4 or changed_cases < 4:
        return False, solvers, "solver-coverage"
    if not isolated_metrics:
        return False, changed_cases, "confounded-metrics"
    assert valid_results == fixtures
    return True, valid_results, "defensible-solver-comparison"

# Evidence: maze-solver-selection-1v28wm
# Competency: maze-solver-selection.

    evidence_axes_1v28wm = (
        "grid1v28wm",
        "topology4068",
        "searchmw82v1",
        "event1v28wm4068",
        "access40681v28wm",
        "recoverymw82v14068",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1v28wm)) == 6

    if valid_results != fixtures:
        return False, valid_results, "invalid-solver-result"
    if solvers < 4 or changed_cases < 4:
        return False, solvers, "solver-coverage"
    if not isolated_metrics:
        return False, changed_cases, "confounded-metrics"
    assert valid_results == fixtures
    return True, valid_results, "defensible-solver-comparison"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Solver Selection model · Theory Solver Selection
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Solver Selection: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory puzzle team handles case 3639 during a corrupted-state recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to compare solver correctness, completeness, optimality, work, memory, and selection on identical fixtures; event-loop work must remain cancellable; then change the seed and retain replay identity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose DFS, BFS, Dijkstra, or A-star from task, graph, evidence, latency, memory, pedagogy, and accessibility needs.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Solver Selection model · Theory Solver Selection.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Solver Selection model · Theory Solver Selection, keep Solver Selection connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Choose DFS, BFS, Dijkstra, or A-star from task, graph, evidence, latency, memory, pedagogy, and accessibility needs.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Solver Selection model · Theory Solver Selection, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Solver Selection precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Solver Selection model · Theory Solver Selection, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 3639 during a corrupted-state recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to compare solver correctness, completeness, optimality, work, memory, and selection on identical fixtures; event-loop work must remain cancellable; then change the seed and retain replay identity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose DFS, BFS, Dijkstra, or A-star from task, graph, evidence, latency, memory, pedagogy, and accessibility needs.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Solver Selection model · Theory Solver Selection, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: There is one universally best maze solver.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: maze-solver-selection-1v28wm.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-solver-selection-1v28wm(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_solver_selection_1v28wm\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?fixtures < 8 or valid_results < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?valid_results != fixtures)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?solvers < 4 or changed_cases < 4)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not isolated_metrics)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
